REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, (PREVIOUSLY TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION ON 9/12/17) CROSS DEFENDANTS BONNIE DONG, MICHAEL GILPIN'S MOTION TO DISMISS COMPLAINT IN INTERVENTION, GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR THE CROSS-COMPLAINT TO JOIN ITS INSURED, A NECESSARY PARTY, AS A CROSS-COMPLAINANT OR AS A CROSS-DEFENDANT. CROSS-COMPLAINT SHALL HAVE 20 DAYS TO FILE AN AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT AND TO SERVE IT PROMPTLY THEREAFTER. OPPOSING PARTY FAILED TO CITE ANY AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT JOINING THE INSURED, A NECESSARY PARTY, UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES, WOULD NOT COMPORT WITH THE POLICY FOR "FAIRNESS, CONVENIENCE AND PRACTICABILITY" TO SUCH AN EXTENT THAT IT WOULD OUTWEIGH THE POLICY FOR "COMPLETE DETERMINATION AND AVOIDANCE OF MULTIPLICITY OF LAWSUITS." SEE PEERLESS INS. V. SUP. CT. (1970) 6 CAL.APP.3D 358 AND PHILLIPS V. WESTERN PAC. R. CO. (1971) 22 CAL.APP.3D 441. PURSUANT TO WRITTEN ORDER. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B).
